skill.name: datamol
skill.description: A Pythonic wrapper around RDKit with simplified interfaces and sensible defaults. Preferred for standard drug discovery workflows including SMILES parsing, standardization, descriptors, fingerprints, clustering, 3D conformer generation, and parallel processing. Returns native rdkit.Chem.Mol objects. For advanced control or custom parameters, use rdkit directly.
skill.summary: Simplified Pythonic interface to RDKit for molecular cheminformatics and drug discovery workflows.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 335 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill contains only Markdown documentation files with code examples for the datamol cheminformatics library, plus one minimal validation script. Detected patterns (weak cryptographic algorithm, shell backtick execution, system reconnaissance, hardcoded URLs) all originate from code examples in documentation, not from executable code. The heuristic dangerous combination finding aggregates these false positive components. No actual security threats exist in this skill.

content.user_title: Run Drug Discovery Molecular Analysis with Datamol
content.value_statement: Running molecular analysis with RDKit requires verbose boilerplate code and deep cheminformatics expertise. Datamol provides simplified Pythonic interfaces with sensible defaults for parsing SMILES, standardizing structures, computing descriptors, generating 3D conformers, and clustering compounds.
actual_capabilities: Parse molecules from SMILES, InChI, SELFIES, and other molecular formats
Standardize and sanitize molecular structures for consistent analysis
Compute molecular descriptors including LogP, molecular weight, TPSA, and H-bond donors
Generate ECFP, FCFP, MACCS, and topological fingerprints for similarity search
Create 3D conformers using ETKDG methods and analyze conformer populations
Cluster molecules using Butina algorithm and select diverse subsets
limitations: Requires datamol and RDKit Python libraries to be installed in the environment
Cannot perform quantum mechanical calculations or molecular dynamics simulations
Butina clustering builds a full distance matrix and is not suitable for more than 10,000 molecules
Visualization output is limited to static image grids with no interactive 3D viewing

use_cases.title: Virtual Screening of Compound Libraries
use_cases.description: A medicinal chemist wants to screen a library of 10,000 compounds for drug-like properties. Use this skill to parse SMILES, compute descriptors, apply Lipinski filters, and select diverse compound subsets for experimental testing.

use_cases.title: Structure-Activity Relationship Analysis
use_cases.description: A computational chemist needs to analyze how small structural changes affect molecular properties. Use this skill to compute descriptors, generate scaffolds, and compare property distributions across compound series.

use_cases.title: Machine Learning Dataset Preparation
use_cases.description: A data scientist needs to prepare a molecular dataset for model training. Use this skill to standardize structures, compute fingerprints as feature vectors, and create scaffold-based train-test splits to avoid data leakage.

prompt_templates.title: Load Molecules and Compute Basic Properties
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the datamol skill to load these SMILES compounds and compute standard molecular descriptors including molecular weight, LogP, H-bond donors, H-bond acceptors, and TPSA for each molecule.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user needs to analyze a small set of compounds

prompt_templates.title: Filter Compounds by Drug-Likeness Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the datamol skill to filter a list of compounds using Lipinski Rule of Five criteria. Compute descriptors for each molecule and return only those that satisfy all four rules: molecular weight under 500, LogP under 5, H-bond donors under 5, and H-bond acceptors under 10.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to identify drug-like candidates from a larger library

prompt_templates.title: Cluster and Select Diverse Compounds
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the datamol skill to cluster these molecules by structural similarity using Butina clustering with a Tanimoto cutoff of 0.3. Then select 50 diverse representative molecules from the clusters for further analysis.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to pick a representative subset from a large compound collection

prompt_templates.title: Generate and Analyze 3D Conformers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the datamol skill to generate 3D conformers for this molecule using the ETKDGv3 method. Generate 100 conformers, filter duplicates with an RMS cutoff of 0.5 angstroms, apply energy minimization, cluster the conformers, and compute solvent-accessible surface area for each cluster centroid.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user needs detailed 3D conformer analysis for molecular modeling

output_examples.input: Load a CSV file of compounds and compute drug-likeness descriptors
output_examples.output: The skill returned a table with 245 compounds and 8 descriptor columns including molecular weight, LogP, H-bond donors and acceptors, TPSA, rotatable bonds, aromatic atoms, and a drug-likeness flag. 183 compounds passed Lipinski Rule of Five.

output_examples.input: Find the 10 most similar compounds to a query molecule from a library
output_examples.output: The skill computed ECFP fingerprints for the query and all library molecules, calculated pairwise Tanimoto similarities, and returned the top 10 matches ranked by similarity score with their SMILES and compound IDs.

best_practices: Always standardize and sanitize user-provided molecules using dm.standardize_mol before analysis to ensure consistent results
Use batch processing with n_jobs=-1 for descriptor computation on large datasets to leverage parallel processing
Use scaffold-based train-test splitting when building machine learning models to prevent data leakage between similar compounds
anti_patterns: Do not use Butina clustering on datasets with more than 10,000 molecules as it builds a full distance matrix and will cause memory errors
Do not skip sanitization of input molecules as malformed SMILES can produce incorrect descriptors and fingerprints
Do not draw scientific conclusions from the built-in toy datasets as they are intended only for testing and learning purposes

faq.question: What is the difference between datamol and RDKit?
faq.answer: Datamol is a lightweight Pythonic wrapper around RDKit. It provides simplified interfaces and sensible defaults for common tasks. RDKit offers more control but requires more verbose code.

faq.question: Do I need to install RDKit separately?
faq.answer: No. RDKit is automatically installed as a dependency when you install datamol. You only need to run 'uv pip install datamol' or 'pip install datamol'.

faq.question: Can datamol read molecular files from cloud storage?
faq.answer: Yes. All I/O functions support remote paths through fsspec integration, including S3, GCS, Azure, and HTTPS endpoints.

faq.question: What fingerprint types does datamol support?
faq.answer: Datamol supports ECFP (Morgan), FCFP, MACCS keys, topological fingerprints, and atom pair fingerprints for similarity calculations.

faq.question: Is datamol suitable for large-scale virtual screening?
faq.answer: Datamol is suitable for screening libraries of thousands to tens of thousands of compounds. For millions of compounds, consider specialized virtual screening platforms.

faq.question: Does datamol support reaction chemistry?
faq.answer: Yes. The datamol.reactions module can apply chemical reactions defined as SMARTS patterns, supporting amide synthesis, Suzuki coupling, esterification, and custom transformations.
